summary: The test run failed because the test_tcl test module had two failing tests; the suite re-ran the failed tests but they failed again, causing the job to be marked as FAILURE.
root_cause: Two tests in the test_tcl test module failed (test_tcl failed (2 failures)), causing the overall test run to fail.

evidence.quote: 0:13:10 load avg: 7.29 [377/505/1] ^[[1;31m^[[1;31mtest_tcl failed (2 failures)^[[0m^[[0m -- running (3): test.test_concurrent_futures.test_shutdown (34.3 sec), test_compileall (1 min 33 sec), test_pickle (59.4 sec)
evidence.reason: Shows the test_tcl module failed with 2 failures during the run.
Indicates there were 2 failures (and some skips) reported for the failing test run.
Shows the exact test command/executable that ran the test suite.

evidence.quote: Re-running 1 failed tests in verbose mode in subprocesses
evidence.reason: Shows the harness attempted to re-run the failing test(s) in verbose mode.
Shows the overall test summary where failures are counted (the job ended with failures).

suggested_fix: Run the failing test module in verbose mode to capture the full traceback and reproduce locally (for example, run D:\a\cpython\cpython\PCbuild\amd64\python_d.exe -m test test_tcl -v), inspect the failing test tracebacks to identify and fix the failing assertions or underlying Tcl-related implementation.
